hypothesis: Replacing the Granite model’s general-purpose tokenizer with a chemistry-aware subword vocabulary trained on MOFid and SMILES, while keeping architecture, pre-training corpora (PMC + 500k PubChem), and SFT protocols fixed, will reduce QMOF band-gap MAE by at least 10% under 1K and 3K SFT settings relative to the original-tokenizer baseline.
